name: Vite
what_it_does: Next-generation frontend build tool that uses native ES modules for an extremely fast development server with hot module replacement (HMR), and bundles production builds with Rollup. Supports React, Vue, Svelte, and vanilla JS/TS out of the box via plugins.
use_cases: Fast local development server with sub-millisecond HMR for frontend projects
Production bundling of React, Vue, Svelte, or vanilla web applications
Library bundling for npm package authors via library mode
Programmatic build orchestration via Node.js API in CI pipelines
Plugin authoring to transform code or assets in the build pipeline
not_for: Server-side rendering without a meta-framework wrapper (use Next.js, Nuxt, or SvelteKit)
Projects that must support IE11 or very old browsers without significant configuration
Replacing webpack in very complex existing webpack configurations without migration effort
best_when: An agent is scaffolding or building a new frontend web project and needs a fast, modern build tool with minimal configuration.
avoid_when: You have an existing complex webpack configuration with many custom loaders that would require significant migration work.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Vite is a local build tool with no cloud service component. The dev server can optionally require HTTPS certificates for local testing.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed open source. No paid tiers, no hosted service, no usage tracking.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Vite build errors are reasonably clear with file paths and line numbers. Transform errors show the problematic code. HMR errors appear in the browser overlay. Rollup errors (used for production builds) can be cryptic with import resolution failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Builds are deterministic given the same source and config. The Node.js API `build()` function is safe to call repeatedly. Output directory is cleaned before rebuild by default.
known_agent_gotchas: vite.config.ts uses different module resolution than the project being built - a common confusion for generated configs
Environment variables must be prefixed with VITE_ to be exposed to client-side code
The dev server and production build can behave differently due to different bundling strategies (unbundled vs Rollup)
Plugin order matters and is not always obvious - agents generating plugin arrays may get order wrong
CSS modules, PostCSS, and preprocessors require specific packages installed even though Vite claims built-in support
SSR mode has significantly different configuration requirements and is often misconfigured

score_rationale: Vite is a high-quality, well-documented build tool but is fundamentally a local CLI/Node.js SDK with no REST API, no hosted service, and no MCP server. Agent-readiness is moderate: the Node.js API is clean and easy to invoke programmatically, configuration is minimal and JSON-like, and documentation is accurate. Score is limited by the lack of any hosted API surface and the fact that errors from the underlying Rollup bundler can be opaque. Excellent choice when an agent needs to scaffold or build a frontend project.
